```python
# test_balanced_parens.py
import pytest

from your_module import is_balanced_parens  # replace with actual module name


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "s",
    [
        "",
        "()",
        "[]",
        "{}",
        "()[]{}",
        "({[]})",
        "{[()]}",
        "((()))",
        "[{()}](){}",
        "abc",
        "a(b)c",
        "function(arg[0]) { return true; }",
        "(hello [world] {test})",
    ],
)
def test_is_balanced_parens_valid_inputs(s):
    assert is_balanced_parens(s) is True


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "s",
    [
        "(",
        "[",
        "{",
        ")",
        "]",
        "}",
        "(]",
        "[)",
        "{]",
        "([)]",
        "({[})",
        "(()",
        "())",
        "{[()]",
        "{[(])}",
        "abc(def]",
        "a)b(c",
    ],
)
def test_is_balanced_parens_invalid_inputs(s):
    assert is_balanced_parens(s) is False


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "s",
    [
        "((a + b) * [c - d])",
        "{foo: [bar, baz]}",
        "no brackets here",
        "12345",
        "!@#$%^&*",
    ],
)
def test_is_balanced_parens_ignores_non_bracket_characters(s):
    assert is_balanced_parens(s) is True


@pytest.mark.parametrize(
    "bad_input",
    [
        None,
        123,
        3.14,
        True,
    ],
)
def test_is_balanced_parens_non_iterable_inputs_raise_type_error(bad_input):
    with pytest.raises(TypeError):
        is_balanced_parens(bad_input)


def test_is_balanced_parens_list_input_iterable_behavior():
    # The function is annotated for str, but it works with any iterable of chars.
    assert is_balanced_parens(["(", "[", "]", ")"]) is True
    assert is_balanced_parens(["(", "[", ")", "]"]) is False
```
